mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that provides vulnerability scanning for container images and file systems. It is useful for ensuring the security of your applications by identifying vulnerabilities in real-time. Key trade-offs include the need for proper RBAC configuration and potential performance overhead during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Aqua Security Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator in the trivy-system namespace.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists the pods in the trivy-system namespace to verify they are running.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration resource
steps.description:
This step creates a TrivyConfiguration custom resource to configure the behavior of the Trivy Operator.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/alpine
    tag: latest
EOF

```

> Creates a default TrivyConfiguration for scanning the Alpine image.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/alpine
    tag: latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify the TrivyConfiguration
steps.description:
This step checks if the TrivyConfiguration resource is ready.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system

```

> Lists the TrivyConfiguration resources to verify the created one is present.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all associated configurations and data.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfiguration-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up the current TrivyConfiguration.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfiguration-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivyconfiguration.trivy.aquasecurity.github.io is forbidden: User "system:serviceaccount:default:default" cannot get resource "trivyconfiguration" in API group "trivy.aquasecurity.github.io"
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the service account does not have the necessary permissions to access the TrivyConfiguration resource.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the appropriate RBAC permissions to access Trivy resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "TrivyConfiguration" in version "trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Trivy CRDs are not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Trivy Operator to ensure CRDs are properly installed.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources allocated to the Trivy Operator pod.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' and adjust resource limits if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Trivy scans are not triggering
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the TrivyConfiguration is not set up correctly or if there are no images to scan.

**Fix:**
Verify the TrivyConfiguration and ensure there are images in the cluster that match the configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image repositories and improved scanning performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from v0.22.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace and the TrivyConfiguration resource listed when running 'kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system'.

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/alpine
    tag: latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
